currency.source: Bank of Zambia (boz.zm), exchangerate.guru, Wise, valutafx, exchange-rates.org
currency.baseNote: Rates show ZMW per 1 unit of foreign currency. ↓ ZMW = Kwacha strengthening (appreciating).
currency.mpRateNote: Monetary Policy Rate reduced 75bps from 14.25% on Feb 11, 2026. BOZ held at 13.5% at May review. Next review: August 2026.

USD.note: exchangerate.guru Jun 8: 17.56. 2026 low was 17.512 on Jun 4 (exchange-rates.org). Wise shows 2.38% decrease in USD/ZMW on Jun 8 — kwacha strengthening resumed.

GBP.note: Cross rate via ZMW/GBP ~0.04264 (Wise Jun 5 high). 1 GBP ≈ 23.45 ZMW.

EUR.note: EUR/ZMW low was 20.263 on Jun 5 (valutafx). 2026 high was 26.069 on Jan 1. -22.27% YTD.

ZAR.note: 1 ZMW = 0.942 ZAR. ZAR/ZMW 2026 high was 1 ZMW = 0.9422 ZAR on Jun 5 (valutafx).

CNY.note: Chinese yuan. Zambia's growing yuan-denominated mining tax corridor.

drivers: Record copper output 890K tonnes (2025) — strong export earnings
Record maize harvest >4M tonnes — reduced food import needs
Debt restructuring 94% complete — sovereign risk premium collapsing
IMF successor programme talks scheduled July 2026 — staff visit confirmed
MPR at 13.5% — high real yields attracting carry trade inflows
Yuan mining tax corridor reducing USD demand
Gross international reserves at historic high of US$6.5 billion
trendAnalysis.outlook: BOZ held MPR at 13.5% at May review. Inflation at 6.8% (April). Further kwacha appreciation possible on copper tailwinds, but BOZ may intervene to protect export competitiveness. Key risks: global copper price reversal, drought cycles, election-related fiscal expansion (Aug 2026).
trendAnalysis.projection3m: USD/ZMW 16.50–18.00 range. Bias toward further appreciation if copper holds above $9,500/tonne and IMF successor programme progresses.
trendAnalysis.projection6m: USD/ZMW 16.00–19.00 range. Wider range reflects election uncertainty (Aug 2026). Post-election policy continuity would support kwacha; fiscal slippage would reverse gains.

news.title: LASI Edges Up 0.03% to 25,698.02 — Low Turnover Monday
news.summary: The LASI rose 8.04 points (+0.03%) to close at 25,698.02. 967 trades across 491,799 shares. Turnover K9.77M. Market cap K333.63B. Breadth negative: 1 advancer (ZNCO +0.05), 3 decliners (CHIL -0.03, PUMA -0.01, CECZ -0.01). Trading in 20 of 24 listed securities.

news.title: Kwacha Resumes Strengthening — USD/ZMW at 17.56, Near 2-Year High
news.summary: The Zambian kwacha strengthened further on June 8, with USD/ZMW hitting 17.56 (down from 18.23 a week ago). Wise reported a 2.38% decrease in USD/ZMW on June 8 — the largest single-day move of the week. The 2026 low remains 17.512 set on June 4. EUR/ZMW at 20.35 (low was 20.263 on Jun 5). ZMW/ZAR at 0.942.

news.title: ZCCM-IH Partners with Wonderful Group for 600MW Coal Power Plant
news.summary: ZCCM Investments Holdings has agreed a deal with Chinese-owned Wonderful Group Services to build a 600 megawatt coal-fired power station, addressing Zambia's energy deficit. The announcement follows the Constitutional Court judgment clearing the Mopani dispute. ZCCM-IH trades at K153.07 with PE 6.5x.

news.title: Zambia Sugar MD Flags Challenging 2026 — 'More Difficult Than Prior Year'
news.summary: Zambia Sugar PLC Country Managing Director Oswald Magwenzi noted that 2026 has been more challenging compared to the previous year, with the company enduring various headwinds in H1 2026. ZSUG closed at K69.09. PE 19.58x, dividend yield 1.59%, FCF negative at -K111.9M.

news.title: OneSquareK Property Development Set to List on LuSE Alternative Market
news.summary: Money Acumen Advisory announced the intention of OneSquareK Property Development Plc (OSK) to list on the Alternative Market of the Lusaka Securities Exchange. Following DCZM's December 2025 debut, OSK would be the second ALT-M listing — described as 'a very different bet' from Dot Com Zambia.

news.title: Zambia GDP Forecast to Expand 6.4% in 2026 — Among Region's Highest
news.summary: Following Zambia's Eurobond restructuring in June 2024, GDP is forecast to expand by 6.4% in 2026, among the region's highest growth rates. The national budget prioritised education, health, and post-drought recovery. The World Bank launched its 2026 Zambia Economic Update on May 8.

news.title: Moore Global Expands into Zambia with Launch of Moore Zambia CFS
news.summary: Moore Global, the international accounting and advisory network, has expanded into Zambia with the launch of Moore Zambia CFS, signaling growing confidence in Zambia's professional services market post-debt restructuring.

news.title: LASI Inches Up 0.96 pts to 25,689.97 — Light Friday Session
news.summary: The Lusaka All Share Index edged up 0.96 points to 25,689.97. 1,025 trades with ATEL dominating value at ZMW 8.12M. ZFCO led gainers (+0.31%). NATB, DCZM, CHIL led decliners.

news.title: BoZ Holds Policy Rate at 13.5% Ahead of August Elections
news.summary: The Bank of Zambia maintained its policy rate at 13.5%, citing stabilizing inflation at 7.5% but cautioning on global energy price risks from the Iran conflict.

news.title: Zambia Posts Record Bumper Harvest — Maize Output Exceeds 4M Tonnes
news.summary: The Ministry of Agriculture confirmed a record maize harvest exceeding 4 million metric tonnes for the 2025/2026 season, driven by favorable rains and expanded FISP coverage.

news.title: Klapton Reinsurance IPO Oversubscribed — Lists March 2026
news.summary: Klapton Reinsurance confirmed its IPO was oversubscribed. The company, with K2.97B in gross written premiums and a Caa1 Moody's rating, is expected to list on LuSE Main Board this month.

news.title: Copper Prices Hold Above $10,000/tonne on AI Data Center Demand
news.summary: Copper prices continue to trade above $10,000/tonne, supported by AI data center buildout and EV manufacturing demand. Zambia's 2025 output hit a record 890,346 metric tonnes.

news.title: IMF Successor Programme Talks Progress — Staff Visit Scheduled July
news.summary: The IMF confirmed a staff visit to Lusaka in July 2026 to begin formal negotiations on a successor programme to the expired ECF. Government emphasizes 'growth-focused agenda.'
